skill.name: azure-mgmt-mongodbatlas-dotnet
skill.description: Manage MongoDB Atlas Organizations as Azure ARM resources using Azure.ResourceManager.MongoDBAtlas SDK. Use when creating, updating, listing, or deleting MongoDB Atlas organizations through Azure Marketplace.
skill.summary: Manage MongoDB Atlas Organizations as Azure ARM resources using the Azure .NET SDK
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill contains only documentation in SKILL.md. The static analyzer flagged 101 potential issues (external_commands, network, weak crypto) but all are false positives. The backticks detected are markdown code formatting, URLs are legitimate documentation links, and the weak crypto patterns are triggered by table cells in markdown. No executable code exists in this skill.

content.value_statement: This skill provides documentation and code examples for managing MongoDB Atlas Organizations as Azure ARM resources using the Azure .NET SDK. It enables developers to integrate MongoDB Atlas with Azure Marketplace for unified billing.
actual_capabilities: Create MongoDB Atlas Organizations as Azure ARM resources
List and retrieve existing Atlas organizations
Handle long-running operations with async patterns
Configure Azure Marketplace subscriptions for Atlas
limitations: Does not manage Atlas clusters, databases, or collections directly
Requires Azure subscription with appropriate RBAC permissions
Only supports MongoDB Atlas Organizations as ARM resources
Requires Azure Marketplace subscription setup for billing

use_cases.title: Integrate MongoDB Atlas with Azure Billing
use_cases.description: Set up MongoDB Atlas organizations through Azure Marketplace for unified billing and resource management.

use_cases.title: Automate Organization Provisioning
use_cases.description: Programmatically create and manage MongoDB Atlas organizations as part of infrastructure-as-code workflows.

use_cases.title: Build Azure-MongoDB Integration Tools
use_cases.description: Develop custom tooling to manage MongoDB Atlas resources alongside other Azure services.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Organization Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to create a MongoDB Atlas organization using the Azure.ResourceManager.MongoDBAtlas SDK with DefaultAzureCredential.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with MongoDB Atlas on Azure

prompt_templates.title: List Organizations
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I list all MongoDB Atlas organizations in a subscription using the Azure .NET SDK?

prompt_templates.title: Update Organization Tags
prompt_templates.prompt: Write code to add and remove tags on a MongoDBAtlasOrganizationResource using the Azure SDK.

prompt_templates.title: Handle Long-Running Operations
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the correct way to handle CreateOrUpdateAsync with WaitUntil.Started and poll for completion.

output_examples.input: How do I create a MongoDB Atlas organization with marketplace details?
output_examples.output: You need to create a MongoDBAtlasOrganizationData with location, marketplace details, and user information. Use DefaultAzureCredential to authenticate, then call CreateOrUpdateAsync on the organization collection.
The SDK uses Azure.Identity for authentication. DefaultAzureCredential is recommended for local development. For production, use ManagedIdentityCredential or ServicePrincipalCredential.

output_examples.input: What properties are required for marketplace subscription?
output_examples.output: You need SubscriptionId, PublisherId (typically 'mongodb'), OfferId, PlanId, PlanName, TermUnit (like 'P1M'), and TermId from your Azure Marketplace subscription.

best_practices: Use async methods for all SDK operations to ensure proper scalability
Always check ProvisioningState before performing operations on created resources
Use ResourceIdentifiers to create resource handles without making API calls
Handle long-running operations with WaitUntil.Completed or implement polling
anti_patterns: Do not use this SDK for managing Atlas clusters directly - use MongoDB Atlas API
Avoid hardcoding credentials - use Azure.Identity credential chain
Do not ignore provisioning state checks after create/update operations

faq.question: What is this SDK used for?
faq.answer: This SDK manages MongoDB Atlas Organizations as Azure ARM resources, enabling unified billing through Azure Marketplace. It does not manage clusters directly.

faq.question: How do I authenticate?
faq.answer: Use Azure.Identity. DefaultAzureCredential is recommended for most scenarios. The SDK supports ManagedIdentityCredential and ServicePrincipalCredential for production.

faq.question: Can I manage Atlas clusters with this SDK?
faq.answer: No. This SDK only manages Atlas Organizations as ARM resources. Use the MongoDB Atlas API for cluster, database, and collection management.

faq.question: What Azure permissions do I need?
faq.answer: You need Contributor or Owner role on the resource group, plus access to the Azure Marketplace offer for your subscription.

faq.question: How does Azure Marketplace billing work?
faq.answer: The SDK configures Marketplace subscription details during organization creation. Billing is handled through your Azure subscription based on the offer details.

faq.question: What is the API version?
faq.answer: This SDK uses API version 2025-06-01 for the MongoDB.Atlas/organizations resource type.
